primary_false_move: path-like or escaped session id treated as lawful authority root
required_boundary: runtime session authority owns identifier hygiene
expected_resolution: reject invalid identity before queue or carrier semantics begin

primary_false_move: queue item treated as dispatchable without lawful readiness or work unit binding
required_boundary: queue/dispatch owns bounded readiness and nomination
expected_resolution: refuse dispatch unless item is DISPATCH_READY and names a work unit

counterexample: api refusal on missing or malformed entry
primary_false_move: api entry treated as if it may mint authority from convenience
required_boundary: api entry attaches to a center but does not replace it
expected_resolution: emit explicit refusal for missing session missing root authority partial context and invalid carrier ref

counterexample: carrier or context conflict under re-attachment pressure
primary_false_move: new api request silently overwrites existing session-side bindings
required_boundary: existing runtime/session bindings remain authoritative until lawfully changed
expected_resolution: refuse conflicting carrier or context attachment

primary_false_move: cancelled queue item treated as still runnable
required_boundary: queue-local status remains authoritative until lawfully changed

primary_false_move: continuation or settlement treated as hidden session authority replacement
required_boundary: pause and re-entry must stay subordinate to runtime/session authority plus continuity law
expected_resolution: emit explicit pause and re-entry handling without center collapse

primary_false_move: settlement used to rewrite runtime center truth
required_boundary: settlement classifies closure but does not own runtime identity or queue history
expected_resolution: keep closure witness subordinate to prior runtime/session receipts
